title.emphasis: The Tiered Engagement Model: From Patronage to Partnership

section.para: This framework allows for three distinct tiers of engagement, each with a corresponding contract combination that reflects the evolving relationship and commitment level.
This is the initial "talent scout" phase, designed to assess an artist's skill and collaborative fit on a low-risk project.

listitem.para: Relationship: Patron-Artist
Contract Used: A simple Statement of Work (SOW) under the standard MSA.
IP Treatment: The artist retains full copyright to their derivative work. The LLC receives a limited, non-commercial license to use the piece for personal enjoyment and display.
Valuation Method: A straightforward project fee, typically paid 50% upfront and 50% on completion.

section.para: For artists who have proven their value, this tier involves creating core assets for the LLC's commercial projects. This stage introduces the "Ledger Contract" concept to establish a transparent valuation of the IP.

listitem.para: Relationship: Producer-Contractor
Contract Used: A detailed "Ledger" SOW under the MSA, which includes a full copyright assignment clause.
IP Treatment: The artist assigns the full copyright to the LLC upon final payment, granting the organization complete ownership for commercial use (a "buyout").
Valuation Method: The SOW acts as a financial ledger to establish a defensible "book value" for the IP. This value is calculated as:
Par Value: The LLC's fixed administrative and legal costs for the commission.
Reimbursed Artist Costs: Documented expenses for the artist's materials and labor (tracked via receipts and timesheets).

section.para: This final stage formalizes the transition of a key collaborator into a vested member of the LLC, using their creative work as their investment.

listitem.para: Relationship: Partner-Member
Contract Used: An IP Contribution Agreement, which serves as an amendment to the LLC Operating Agreement.
IP Treatment: The artist's high-value IP is formally transferred to the LLC as a capital contribution in exchange for a membership interest (equity).
Valuation Method: The "Book Value" established in the Tier 2 Ledger Contract provides a transparent, agreed-upon valuation for the IP being contributed as capital.

row.entry: Artist retains copyright; LLC gets a limited, non-commercial license.
Artist assigns full copyright to the LLC ("buyout").
IP Contribution Agreement (Amends LLC Operating Agreement)
Artist's IP is contributed to the LLC as a capital investment.
Agreed-upon value, often based on the "Book Value" from Tier 2.

section.para: By integrating these contractual layers, the venture can operate with legal clarity and strategic purpose. This ecosystem allows relationships to mature naturally, providing a clear and defensible pathway for artists to grow from commissioned creators into true equity partners in the collective enterprise.

listitem.para: MSA vs SOW: Which One To Use When - Ironclad, accessed August 28, 2025, https://ironcladapp.com/journal/contracts/msa-vs-sow/

Distinguishing between a master service agreement and a statement of works - HCR Law, accessed August 28, 2025, https://www.hcrlaw.com/news-and-insights/distinguishing-between-a-master-services-agreement-and-a-statement-of-works/
